name: FIBROUS CORTICAL DEFECT
definition: developmental abnormality classified as benign bone tumor

demographics.Histo: fibrous tissue from periosteum invading underlying cortex
demographics.Location: metaphyseal cortex of long bone (near physis); multiple locations bilateral +

dominant_finding: smooth lobulated / scalloped margins with well-defined thin rim of sclerosis
distribution: metaphyseal cortex of long bone (near physis); multiple locations bilateral +
discriminating_features: circular / oval eccentrically located radiolucent area extending parallel to long axis of host
smooth lobulated / scalloped margins with well-defined thin rim of sclerosis
high SI on T2WI (cystic component early stage) + low SI on T1WI
little / no uptake on bone scintigraphy

general: circular / oval eccentrically located radiolucent area extending parallel to long axis of host
smooth lobulated / scalloped margins with well-defined thin rim of sclerosis
high SI on T2WI (cystic component early stage) + low SI on T1WI
little / no uptake on bone scintigraphy
low SI on T2WI (progressive sclerosis + ossification) and on T1WI
sclerotic bone island (may be residue of incompletely involuted cortical defect in the adult)
